label: Naypyidaw
sub: SAC Junta Capital — Military administrative center; site of SAC command; largely isolated from conflict

label: Yangon
sub: Myanmar's largest city — CDM epicenter 2021; mass protests; continued SAC control; economic deterioration

label: Mandalay
sub: Myanmar's second city — Earthquake devastation (Mar 2025); PDF pressure in surrounding townships; SAC still holds city center

label: Lashio
sub: Northern Shan State capital — Captured by MNDAA/TNLA Aug 2024; first Regional Military Command HQ lost by junta

label: Laukkai
sub: Kokang capital — Captured by MNDAA Jan 5, 2024; thousands of online scam compound victims freed

label: Chin Shwe Haw
sub: Strategic Myanmar-China border crossing — Captured by MNDAA Nov 2023; cut SAC border revenue and supply routes

label: Sittwe
sub: Rakhine State capital — Under AA siege; one of last SAC holdouts in Rakhine; humanitarian crisis for trapped civilians

label: Myitkyina
sub: Kachin State capital — KIA pressing SAC outskirts; SAC increasingly isolated; major humanitarian crisis in region

label: Loikaw
sub: Kayah State capital — Karenni forces have partially surrounded city; SAC relies on airlifts; civilians trapped

label: Myawaddy
sub: Karen State border town — KNLA briefly captured Apr 2024; strategic trade corridor with Thailand; contested

label: Hpa-An
sub: Karen State capital — KNU/KNLA operations in surrounding areas; SAC under pressure; active frontlines nearby

label: Ann Township
sub: Former Western Regional Military Command HQ — Captured by AA Dec 20, 2024; devastating strategic loss for junta

label: Maungdaw
sub: Northern Rakhine — Captured by AA Dec 2024; AA now controls full 270-km Bangladesh border; Rohingya crisis epicenter

label: Mrauk-U
sub: Ancient Rakhine capital — AA captured entire district by Feb 2024; UNESCO heritage sites at risk during conflict

label: Paletwa
sub: Chin State — AA captured Jan 2024; controls Kaladan River corridor linking to India's transit project

label: Sagaing Region
sub: PDF heartland — Most villages burned campaign; highest number of airstrike incidents; over 800 villages burned by SAC

label: Kalay
sub: Sagaing Region — Chin-Sagaing border PDF stronghold; major PDF operations base; CDF and Sagaing PDF cooperation

label: Hakha
sub: Chin State capital — CDF controls surrounding areas; SAC garrison largely isolated; active resistance operations

label: Depayin (Kanbalu)
sub: SAC airstrike Oct 23, 2022 — Bombed charity concert killing 80+ civilians; one of deadliest single air attacks

label: Pazigyi Village
sub: SAC airstrike Apr 11, 2023 — 170+ killed at inauguration ceremony; UN Special Rapporteur called it 'act of pure terror'

label: Hpruso
sub: Christmas Eve Massacre Dec 24, 2021 — SAC forces burned 35+ civilians alive in vehicles; HRW documented as war crime

label: Lay Kay Kaw
sub: KNU liberated zone — Model resistance-administered town near Thai border; KNU provides civil governance and services

label: Mong Ko
sub: Northern Shan — Captured by MNDAA in early days of Op 1027; strategic border town controlling trade routes

label: Taunggyi
sub: Southern Shan State capital — SAC holdout but under pressure from RCSS/SSA and PDF forces in surrounding townships

label: Kyaukpyu
sub: Chinese-backed deep-sea port — One of last SAC holdouts in Rakhine; China concerned about project security; AA surrounds

label: Taungup
sub: Southern Rakhine — AA captured after 42-day siege Dec 2024; falls on key highway connecting Rakhine to Bago Region

label: Demoso
sub: Kayah State — Karenni resistance stronghold; KNDF/KA base; heavy SAC bombing of civilian areas documented

label: Bago
sub: Bago Region — Apr 9, 2021 massacre: SAC killed 80+ protesters; one of bloodiest single-day protests crackdowns

label: Let Yet Kone
sub: Kachin State — Apr 2022 airstrike on school/IDP camp killed 60+ civilians; UN called for accountability

label: Muse
sub: Northern Shan — Major China trade gateway; contested; Operation 1027 threatened key Muse-Mandalay highway trade

label: Shwe Min Gan Naval Base
sub: Sittwe, Rakhine — AA assault launched Mar 23, 2026; 30–40 junta troops killed; AA fighters advanced to within 2 km of Sittwe city center

label: Magway Air Base
sub: Magway Region — Primary SAC airstrike hub for Upper Myanmar; targeted by BWM/NMD-PDF rocket attack (11 x 107mm rounds) Mar 25, 2026

label: Ayardaw Township
sub: Sagaing Region — SAC jets killed 6+ civilians in March 22, 2026 airstrikes; part of surge killing 27+ Sagaing civilians in 3 days

label: Padan Township
sub: Magway Region — AA and resistance attacked junta Nat Yay Kan hilltop base Mar 29, 2026; junta responded with artillery and airstrikes on nearby villages

label: Butalin Township
sub: Sagaing Region — 3 SAC jets killed ~10 civilians in Ngapayin village Mar 30, 2026; 4 bombs + rockets over 75 minutes; no resistance presence reported

label: Hpakant Township
sub: Kachin State jade-mining heartland — KIA captured 6 SAC frontline positions near Maw Wan Gyi on Apr 2, 2026; ongoing KIA offensive threatening SAC junta control and jade revenue

label: Myaing–Pauk Oil Fields
sub: Magway Region — SAC jets killed 5 PDF fighters on Apr 4, 2026 after PDF surrounded junta-controlled oil fields; junta using air power to defend petroleum assets

label: Kani Township
sub: Sagaing Region — Active PDF stronghold; sustained resistance ambushes on SAC convoys and positions; Apr 5, 2026 clashes part of ongoing campaign; 17+ SAC troops killed Apr 5–6

label: Natmauk Township
sub: Magway Region — PDF expanding from Sagaing heartland into Magway; Apr 5, 2026 resistance clashes killed multiple SAC troops in coordination with Kani Township operations

label: Shwebo Township
sub: Sagaing Region — SAC jets conducted 7 bombing runs on IDP displacement camp Apr 7, 2026; monk teaching displaced children killed; 2 women severely injured

label: Wuntho Township
sub: Northern Sagaing Region — SAC airstrikes on villages Apr 9, 2026; part of near-daily aerial campaign across Sagaing while junta chief prepared for 'civilian' inauguration

label: Ye Township
sub: Southern Mon State — PDF ambush Apr 7, 2026 killed 1 SAC officer + 2 soldiers; Ramanya Mon Army also attacked SAC in adjacent Mudon Township same day

label: Minbu-Saku Area
sub: Magway Region — 6+ days of sustained resistance vs. SAC combat as of Apr 7, 2026; school bombed; resistance claims 10+ SAC troops injured; part of PDF push south from Sagaing

label: Launglon
sub: Tanintharyi Region — Launglone Steel Butterfly Force drone strike on junta police station Apr 10, 2026: 2 killed, 7 seriously injured; southern resistance expanding precision drone operations

label: Bilin Township
sub: Mon State — Sustained SAC airstrikes Apr 7-11, 2026: ~40 bombs dropped on Lay Kay, Win Ta Pan, and Myit Kyo village tracts; telecom towers destroyed; resistance besieging Laykay base and Wintarpan military camp

label: Chaungkan Village (Pale Township)
sub: Sagaing Region — Three civilians found tortured and executed Apr 12, 2026 after being abducted as human shields during SAC operations; evidence of torture, gunshot wounds, decapitation

label: Seik Hkun Monastery (W. Shwebo)
sub: Sagaing Region — SAC jets bombed monastery on Thingyan eve Apr 13, 2026: 2 monks killed, 8 novice monks injured; dormitories, wells, and school destroyed

label: Salingyi / Yinmabin (Sagaing)
sub: Sagaing Region — SAC gyrocopter attacks killed 1 infant (Weikwe village, Salingyi Twp) and 1 girl age 14 (Leingnaut village, Yinmabin Twp) on Thingyan eve Apr 13, 2026

label: North Lukan Village (Pakokku)
sub: Magway Region — SAC forces burned ~200 homes on Thingyan eve Apr 13, 2026; ~5 miles from Pakokku airfield; residents had evacuated before burning

label: Pauk Township (Magway)
sub: Magway Region — SAC airstrikes during Myanmar New Year (Apr 17, 2026) killed 6 civilians in Pauk Township and adjacent Luthaw area; SAC maintained operations through entire Thingyan holiday
